purpose: Make classroom HTML products look polished for students while making the teacher look prepared, credible, organized, and current.
rules: Separate teacher controls, answer keys, telemetry, and setup panels from the student-facing path.
Use clean hierarchy and labels so a projected or screenshotted teacher dashboard reads as professional instructional evidence.
Never expose debug labels, placeholder strings, raw JSON, or developer jokes in teacher-facing surfaces.
Provide shareable summaries with concise learning-language, not system-language.
Prefer card/group layouts that make next instructional action obvious at a glance.
